expressions.korean: 문을 닫다
expressions.english: to close the door; to go out of business

expressions.korean: 뚜껑을 닫다
expressions.english: to close the lid

expressions.korean: 창문을 닫다
expressions.english: to close the window

expressions.korean: 문 닫고 들어오세요.
expressions.english: Please come in and close the door.

expressions.korean: 은행은 몇 시에 문을 닫아요?
expressions.english: What time does the bank close?
expressions.notes:
은행 = bank
몇 시 = what time

expressions.korean: 추워요, 창문 좀 닫아 주세요.
expressions.english: It's cold, please close the window.
expressions.notes: 춥다 = (place) to be cold; to feel cold

expressions.korean: 더운데 왜 창문을 닫아 뒀어요?
expressions.english:
Why do you keep the window closed even though it's hot?
It's hot, so why is the window still closed?
expressions.notes:
왜 = why; for what reason; how come
-이/여 도다 = to leave something in a certain state

expressions.korean: 그 가게는 밤 11시에 문을 닫아요.
expressions.english: That store closes at 11 at night.

expressions.korean: 펜을 다 쓰면 뚜껑을 꼭 닫아 주세요.
expressions.english: When you finish using the pen, please make sure to put the cap back on.
expressions.notes:
펜 = pen
쓰다 = to use; to spend (+ money)
꼭 = for sure; at any cost; certainly; definitely; make sure to (do something); tight

expressions.korean: 제가 좋아하는 식당이 문을 닫았어요.
expressions.english: The restaurant that I like went out of business.
expressions.notes:
식당 = restaurant; cafeteria
식당 means "restaurant", but the word "레스토랑" refers to fancier or more upscale restaurants than "식당."

expressions.korean: 물병의 뚜껑을 꼭 안 닫아서 물이 쌀어요.
expressions.english: Since I didn't close the cap on the water bottle tightly, the water leaked out.
expressions.notes:
물병 = water bottle
꼭 = fully; tight
물 = water
새다 = to leak

expressions.korean: 여기는 문을 닫았으니까 다른 곳으로 가요.
expressions.english: This place is closed, let's go to a different place.

expressions.korean: 냄비 뚜껑을 닫아 두면 물이 더 빨리 끓어요.
expressions.english: If you keep the lid of the pot closed, the water boils faster.

expressions.korean: 나오기 전에 창문 다 닫았는지 확인해 주세요.
expressions.english: Before going out, please check if you closed all the windows.
expressions.notes:
-ㅣ/여/았는지 = probably because + S + V-ed; whether + S + V-ed
확인하다 = to check

expressions.korean: 아기가 자고 있으니까 문을 조심히 닫아 주세요.
expressions.english: Since the baby is sleeping, please carefully close the door.
expressions.notes:
아기 = baby
자다 = to sleep
조심하다 = to be careful
조심히 is the adverb form of 조심하다.

expressions.korean: 바람 들어오니까 들어올 때 문을 꼭 닫아 주세요.
expressions.english: Since the wind is coming in, close the door all the way when you come in.
expressions.notes:
바람 = wind
-(으)ㄹ 때 = when/while + S + V

expressions.korean: 문 닫지 마세요. 환기시키려고 열어 놀은 거예요.
expressions.english: Don't close the door. I kept it open to circulate the air.
expressions.notes:
환기시키다 = to circulate the air
-(으)려고 = in order to + V
열다 = to open

expressions.korean: 그 가게는 인테리어 공사를 하려고 한 달간 문을 닫는대요.
expressions.english:
I heard that store is closing for a month to have the interior renovated.
They say that store is closing for one month to have interior construction.
expressions.notes:
인테리어 = interior
공사 = construction
공사를 하다 = to do construction work; to construct
(amount of time) + -간 = for, during
-대요 = I heard that + S + V; They say + S + V

expressions.korean: 어제 문 닫는 걸 잊었나 봐요. 아침에 오니까 문이 열려 있었어요.
expressions.english: I must have forgotten to close the door yesterday. The door was open when I came in the morning.
expressions.notes:
잊다 = to forget
-우/았나 봐요 = S + must have + p.p.; It seems like + S + V-ed
-이/여 있다 is used to express the continuing state of a completed action (past continuous).

expressions.korean: 가게에 손님이 오셨는데 문 닫을 시간이 다 되어서 돌려보냈어요.
expressions.english: Customers came to the store, but since it was time to close, I sent them back out.
expressions.notes:
시간 = time; hour
시간이 되다 = time is up
돌려보내다 = to send someone back
